DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-27-2018 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Neil Swartzberg, Esq. 415-400-7711 of Pearson Simon Warshaw LLP for Plaintiff Stephen Davis.
Nicole Healy, Esq. 650-780-1733 of RMKB for Defendant ROCKET LAWYER INC., CHARLES MOORE.
Christopher Adishian, Esq. 310-726-0888 of Adishian Law Group P.C. for ACENDI INTERACTIVE COMPANY.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court hears both demurrers. Defendant Rocket Lawyer argues breach of contract. Plaintiff responds. Defendant Ascendi argues reliance argument. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROCKET LAWYER INC., CHARLES MOORE'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND EQUITABLE RELIEF.

Rocket Lawyer, Inc. and Charles Moore's demurrers to the second amended complaint (SAC) are OVERRULED; the complaint adequately pleads facts as directed by the Court's December 6, 2017 order. SAC paragraphs 43-68 adequately plead equitable estoppel. The SAC also adequately pleads that Rocket Lawyer ratified the contract and a breach of fiduciary duty based on shareholder status.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Neil Swartzberg, Esq. 415-400-7711 of Pearson Simon Warshaw LLP for Plaintiff Stephen Davis.
Nicole Healy, Esq. 650-780-1733 of RMKB for Defendant ROCKET LAWYER INC., CHARLES MOORE.
Christopher Adishian, Esq. 310-726-0888 of Adishian Law Group P.C. for ACENDI INTERACTIVE COMPANY.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. Parties submit to the tentative ruling.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROCKET LAWYER INC., CHARLES MOORE'S MOTION TO STRIKE CLAIMS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND ATTORNEY'S FEES FROM PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Moore's motion to strike the SAC's punitive damages prayer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff must plead facts in the body of his complaint alleging that Moore acted with malice, oppression or fraud when breaching his fiduciary duty. Moore's motion to strike the attorney fees prayer is OVERRULED. Delaware law appears to apply, as Rocket Lawyer and Acendi are Delaware entities and at least one agreement says Delaware law applies. Moore cites no authority for his notion that attorney fees are a procedural issue governed by California law.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/RBU)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-27-2018 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Neil Swartzberg, Esq. 415-400-7711 of Pearson Simon Warshaw LLP for Plaintiff Stephen Davis.
Nicole Healy, Esq. 650-780-1733 of RMKB for Defendant ROCKET LAWYER INC., CHARLES MOORE.
Christopher Adishian, Esq. 310-726-0888 of Adishian Law Group P.C. for ACENDI INTERACTIVE COMPANY.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court hears both demurrers. Defendant Rocket Lawyer argues breach of contract. Plaintiff responds. Defendant Ascendi argues reliance argument. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ACENDI INTERACTIVE COMPANY, LLC'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Acendi Interactive Company, LLC's demurrers to the SAC are OVERRULED; they repeat the arguments made by Rocket Lawyer and Moore; the complaint adequately pleads facts as directed by the Court's December 6, 2017 order. SAC paragraphs 43-68 adequately plead equitable estoppel. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/RBU)

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT, ACENDI INTERACTIVE COMPANY, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is
Granted without leave to amend. No substantive opposition filed. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Newton Lam: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, e-mail: ssawyer@sftc.org. (L&M302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, Neil Swartzberg and Alexander L. Simon of the law offices of Pearson Simon Warshaw LLP (415-400-7703) appear as counsels for plaintiff/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS ROCKET LAWYER INC. and CHARLES MOORE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to causes of action 1 - 4 and 6. Plaintiff has leave to plead facts showing that defendants are equitably estopped from invoking the statute of limitations as to plaintiff's claims. "When a ground for objection to a complaint, such as the statute of limitations, appears on its face or from matters of which the court may or must take judicial notice, a demurrer on that ground is proper." (Hightower v. Roman Catholic Bishop of Sacramento (2006) 142 Cal.App.4th 759, 765.) Paragraph 28 of the amended complaint alleges that by December 2010, Mr. Moore did not recognize plaintiff's right to receive the Rocket Lawyer interest and refused to document that interest. The breach occurred at this time. There are no allegations that defendants were supposed to perform at a later date and the course of conduct up to that time and plaintiff's requests for documentation indicate that performance was supposed to occur by December of 2010. Plaintiff correctly notes that a defendant can be estopped from relying on the statute of limitations where the defendant lulls the plaintiff into forbearing on bringing the claim. "[I]f a plaintiff can show, in a particular case, that the defendant's promises or attempts to repair prevented a timely suit, the defendant may be equitably estopped from invoking the protection of the statute of limitations." Lantzy v. Centex Homes, (2003) 31 Cal.4th 363, 383 (emphasis in original). Here, plaintiff does not allege sufficient facts to support equitable estoppel. The allegations that refer to defendants conduct after the December 2010 breach (pars. 29-32) do not clearly show an estoppel. Plaintiff has leave to plead more facts regarding how he was lulled into forbearance after December of 2010 and when he learned that defendants would not perform. Plaintiff also has leave to amend to allege that the breach of fiduciary duty claim is based on his shareholder-not warrant owner-status and that Rocket Lawyer, Inc. ratified the parties' agreement. The amended complaint sufficiently alleges the terms of the agreement and that Mr. Moore was acting for his self-interest. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Newton Lam: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, e-mail: ssawyer@sftc.org. (L&M302)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, Neil Swartzberg and Alexander L. Simon of the law offices of Pearson Simon Warshaw LLP (415-400-7703) appear as counsels for plaintiff/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS ROCKET LAWYER INC. and CHARLES MOORE'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is off calendar as moot.

Judge: Newton Lam: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, e-mail: ssawyer@sftc.org. (L&M302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, Neil Swartzberg and Alexander L. Simon of the law offices of Pearson Simon Warshaw LLP (415-400-7703) appear as counsels for plaintiff/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT, ACENDI INTERACTIVE COMPANY, LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to causes of action 1 - 4 for failing to state a cause of action. Plaintiff has leave to plead facts showing that defendants are equitably estopped from invoking the statute of limitations as to plaintiff's claims. "When a ground for objection to a complaint, such as the statute of limitations, appears on its face or from matters of which the court may or must take judicial notice, a demurrer on that ground is proper." (Hightower v. Roman Catholic Bishop of Sacramento (2006) 142 Cal.App.4th 759, 765.) Paragraph 28 of the amended complaint alleges that by December 2010, Mr. Moore did not recognize plaintiff's right to receive the Rocket Lawyer interest and refused to document that interest. The breach occurred at this time. There are no allegations that defendants were supposed to perform at a later date and the course of conduct up to that time and plaintiff's requests for documentation indicate that performance was supposed to occur by December of 2010. Plaintiff correctly notes that a defendant can be estopped from relying on the statute of limitations where the defendant lulls the plaintiff into forbearing on bringing the claim. "[I]f a plaintiff can show, in a particular case, that the defendant's promises or attempts to repair prevented a timely suit, the defendant may be equitably estopped from invoking the protection of the statute of limitations." Lantzy v. Centex Homes, (2003) 31 Cal.4th 363, 383 (emphasis in original). Here, plaintiff does not allege sufficient facts to support equitable estoppel. The allegations that refer to defendants conduct after the December 2010 breach (pars. 29-32) do not clearly show an estoppel. Plaintiff has leave to plead more facts regarding how he was lulled into forbearance after December of 2010 and when he learned that defendants would not perform. The demurrer to cause of action 5 for inspection is overruled. AIC fails to show that plaintiff as a shareholder lacks the power to compel an inspection and plaintiff's status as a shareholder, member, or both is a factual issue not amenable to resolution on demurrer. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Newton Lam: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, e-mail: ssawyer@sftc.org. (L&M302)
